profile.summary: Secondary school Mathematics teacher with 8 years of classroom experience and 2 years as a department lead. Taught classes of up to 32 students and improved the proportion achieving a grade 5 or above in GCSE Maths from 61 to 78 percent over three years.

experiences.title: Lead Teacher of Mathematics
experiences.description: Subject leadership for Key Stages 3 and 4 alongside a full teaching timetable.
achievements: Improved the proportion of students achieving a grade 5 or above in GCSE Maths from 61 to 78 percent over three years.
Led a department of 5 teachers and mentored 2 early-career teachers through their induction year.
Designed a Key Stage 3 intervention program that raised average end-of-year scores by 12 percentage points.

experiences.title: Mathematics Teacher
experiences.description: Full-time classroom teacher across Key Stages 3 and 4.
achievements: Taught 5 classes of up to 32 students each while maintaining a 96 percent attendance rate in lessons.
Introduced low-stakes weekly quizzing that lifted average assessment results by 9 percent across two cohorts.

jobPosting.title: Head of Mathematics Department
jobPosting.fullText: Harrogate Grange School is an Ofsted-rated 'Outstanding' secondary school with around 1,100 students on roll. We are seeking a Head of Mathematics Department to start in the new academic year. Responsibilities: lead a department of 7 teachers, set the curriculum and assessment strategy across Key Stages 3 to 5, monitor pupil progress and raise attainment, and line-manage and develop the team. Requirements: Qualified Teacher Status, several years of secondary Maths teaching experience, and evidence of raising attainment, ideally with prior curriculum or subject-leadership experience. We offer a supportive leadership team, a generous CPD allowance, and a clear progression pathway. Your point of contact is Mrs. Helen Marsh, Deputy Headteacher. Please state your earliest possible start date in your application.
